programmes.show_description: Culture Club emerged in the 80s UK new romantic scene with iconic style and music. Explore their journey and influence, highlighting the unexpected romance within the band. 2025.
Kate Mosse and Emma Dabiri: At the 2023 Hay Festival, authors Kate Mosse and Emma Dabiri sit down with Simon Savidge to discuss their books, lives and inspirations. (S1, ep 3)
Andi Oliver and Elizabeth Day host as members of Idle Readers book club from Manchester meet Ian Rankin. Plus, Delia Owens Zooms in from across the pond. (4 of 6)

programmes.show_name: Inside Art: Lubaina Himid At Tate Modern
programmes.show_description: Kate Bryan visits the studio of the artist Lubaina Himid, winner of the 2017 Turner Prize, before exploring her new large-scale exhibition at Tate Modern.

programmes.show_name: Inside Art: Night Fever At V&A Dundee
programmes.show_description: Kate Bryan explores a new exhibition that explores the relationship between club culture and design traversing the globe and spanning the 1960s to the present day.
Candoco Dance Company: A series of fascinating shorts taking you deeper into the world of art and the people behind it.

programmes.show_name: Cirque Du Soleil: Kurios - Cabinet of Curiosities
programmes.show_description: Discover a world of wonders in the Cabinet of Curiosities, a steampunk-inspired spectacle that asks you to close our eyes and trust your imagination.

The Boy Who Talked With Animals: When David rescues a giant turtle, it looks as though he's found a new friend. But there could be more to his pet than meets the eye. (S4, ep 3)
Best of Everything: Desperate to win the affections of his boss's daughter, Arthur accepts the help of his friend Charlie. But is he ready to count the true cost of love? (S4, ep 4)
The Manacled: Shackled prisoner Stephen Fontaine tries to negotiate an escape from Sergeant Rockwell while en route to San Quentin. (S2, 18)
A Bottle of Wine: Judge Connors shares a civilized conversation with the young architect about to steal his wife. But the quality of his mercy is strained. (S2, ep 19)
A look at the life and career of the director of Zero Dark Thirty and The Hurt Locker, the latter of which she won the Oscar for Best Director. (S6, ep 5)
A look at the life and career of the Oscar-nominated actor, known for his star turn as Sherlock Holmes and roles in David Copperfield and The Adventures of Robin Hood. (S11, ep 6)
Woman's Help: Henpecked Arthur decides to get rid of his demanding wife once and for all, so long as he gets her money. Unfortunately, his wife has a few secrets of her own... (S4, ep 5)
Shatterproof: Ellen is trapped in a loveless marriage with her wealthy husband. But her plan to kill him backfires when the hitman she hires becomes friendly with the target. (S4, ep 6)
...Year: The Exhibition 2025. New. Take a trip down memory lane with a look at this exhibition celebrating some of the finest art to have come out of Sky Arts' Portrait Artist Of The Year.
New. The Birth of Venus: Dive into Botticelli's Birth of Venus, uncovering its mythic roots, artistic brilliance and lasting power as an icon of beauty and imagination. (S1, ep 5)
New. The Last Supper: Explore the genius behind Da Vinci's Last Supper, from its bold perspective to the mysteries and theories that keep this masterpiece legendary. (S1, ep 6)
A look at the life of one of Hollywood's most successful directors, whose long list of hit films include Pulp Fiction and Once Upon a Time in Hollywood. (S6, ep 6)
He's an Academy Award-winning actor who starred in Deliverance and Coming Home, and the father of Hollywood star Angelina Jolie. This is the story of Jon Voight. (S11, ep 7)
Sound Machine: Klausner is a botanist who has invented a machine that can translate the sounds of trees and plants into intelligible speech, but he is shocked by what he hears. (S4, ep 7)
Never Speak Ill of the Dead: David is accused of murder when his wife Irene vanishes. Her promiscuous lifestyle made her the talk of the town, but the gossip has escalated. (S4, ep 8)
Malice Domestic: Marital discord between writer Carl Borden and his wife Annette intensifies when poison is found in his food. (S2, ep 20)
Number Twenty-Two: Young robber Steve Morgan is in jail for his first offense and too cocky for his own good. (S2, ep 21)
A look at the life of the three-time Academy Award-winner, whose fruitful career includes acclaimed roles in My Left Foot, Gangs of New York and There Will Be Blood. (S12, ep 9)
A screenwriter develops a dangerous relationship with a faded film star determined to make a triumphant return. (1950)
A glimpse into the life and career of the filmmaker whose portfolio of work includes Vera Cruz, The Big Knife, The Dirty Dozen and The Longest Yard. (S5, ep 10)
Rocker Billy Idol's journey from underground star to pop icon unfolds in a documentary chronicling his fame, drug use and powerful return to music.
Immerse yourself in one of the most stunning rock and roll concerts of all time, as INXS perform live in front of 74,000 fans at Wembley Stadium in the summer of 1991.

programmes.show_name: National Treasures - The Art Of Collecting
programmes.show_description: The great art collectors of the world reveal their most valuable treasures, starting with a collector of Islamic art who has amassed 35,000 pieces. (S1, ep 1)

programmes.show_name: Inside Art: Picasso/Ingres At the National Gallery
programmes.show_description: Take a look at two portraits, Picasso's 'Woman with a Book' and 'Madame Moitessier' by Jean-Auguste-Dominique Ingres, side by side, for the first time.
Enjoy an exhilarating performance of the 1975 Broadway musical The Wiz with a modern twist - a soul and R&B modern interpretation of the classic Wizard of Oz tale.

National treasure Dame Judi Dench invites her friend, Sir Kenneth Branagh, for tea at her Surrey home. They swap star-studded stories from their careers on stage and screen.

programmes.show_name: Michael Flatley Lord Of The Dance: Dangerous Games
programmes.show_description: Choreographed by Michael Flatley, 40 of the world's most talented performers combine the best of traditional and contemporary music and dance.

programmes.show_name: Electric Light Orchestra: Live - The Early Years
programmes.show_description: A compilation of three ELO concerts: Fusion Live in London (1976), plus unreleased shows at Brunel University (1973) and Germany's Rockpalast (1974).

programmes.show_name: Paul McCartney And Wings: One Hand Clapping
programmes.show_description: Filmed at Abbey Road Studios in 1974 on videotape, experience the inner workings of the band as they perform together.
Mark Knopfler and the band perform songs from their final album On Every Street alongside their greatest hits. Concert film capturing Dire Straits during their last world tour in 1992.

programmes.show_name: Mark Knopfler: A Life in Songs
programmes.show_description: A detailed profile of the life and career of musician Mark Knopfler.
